question.text: Which of the following best describes the claim structure of equity shareholders on a company?

answer.text: Priority claim on assets before debt holders
Fixed claim on periodic interest payments
Residual claim on net assets after all liabilities are paid

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Equity investors have a residual claim on the company's net assets — meaning they receive whatever remains after all liabilities (including debt obligations) have been paid. They are not entitled to fixed payments or priority claims. **The Trap:** Some candidates confuse equity with preference shares — preference shares have priority over equity but still come after debt. **Cross-Exam Context:** Claim hierarchy (debt > preference > equity) is fundamental and tested across X-A, XII, VIII, and IX. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This residual claim nature is precisely why equity offers higher potential returns but also higher risk — the risk-return tradeoff is built into the claim structure.
